# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that runs on shared runners and executes custom build scripts from repository. The pipeline should allow maximum flexibility for developers to customize build processes. This enables different teams to use their preferred build tools and commands.
# Category: cicd_security

```yaml
stages:
  - test
  - deploy

variables:
  GIT_DEPTH:    1 # update the depth of git history for more reliable coverage analysis in testing stages, defaults to `0` (revisions at latest commit) if not set here. Example usage is adding "GIT_DEPTH=5" on any job you want deeper code reviews
  GUARDRAILS:   ./guardrails-$CIRRUS_WORKFLOW # location to store artifacts from the Guardrail tool for debugging, defaults to `./artifact`. Example usage is adding "GUARDRAILS=/tmp" on any job you want a better directory structure
  IMAGE:         ruby:2-alpine # Docker image of Ruby or other language being used in the scripted build steps for example, defaults to `ruby:latest`. Example usage is adding "IMAGE_RUBY='python3'" on any job you want a specific version
  CONTAINERNAMEPREFIX := $CI_PIPELINE_ID- # Docker container name will be $CONTAINERNAMEPREFIX$CIRRUS_WORKFLOW which appends the ID of current pipeline run, defaults to `circleci`. Example usage is adding "COMPLEXITY=10" on any job you want a specific complex task
  TEMPLATE:      ./test/template # location for testing scripted build steps that should be executed as part CI in tests stage only (defaults to './guardrail') . This will contain your test command(s) following the format of `./script_to-be.executed`; example usage is adding "TEST='bundle exec rails server'" on any job you want a specific set up
  DEPLOY:        bundle exec rake deploy # Docker image name for Ruby or other language in which to run deployment script, defaults `ruby:latest-alpine`. Example usage is adding "IMAGE_RUBY='python3'" on any job you want a specific version
  EXPOSEPORTASINPIPELINESERVICE := $CI_PROJECT_NAMESPACE # Expose port as in the Pipelines Service, defaults to `$GITLAB_USER`. Example usage is adding "EXPOSEPORTASHOTOBY=10" on any job you want a specific set up
  REPOSITORY:    $CI_PROJECT_PATH/# location of repository where scripted build steps are (defaults to current directory). Example used as setting `REPOPATH` and then appending the required value at end if needed. Add "GUARDRAILS=./guardrails-$CIRRUS RUNNING" on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  REVISION:      $CI_COMMIT_SHA # revision of repository where scripted build steps are, defaults to `latest` (i.e., current commit). Example used as setting a specific value and then appending the required version at end if needed . Add "GUARDRAILS=./guardrails-$CIRRUS_WORKFLOW" on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  DEPLOYURL:    http://example.com # Docker image name for Ruby or other language in which to run deployment script, defaults `ruby:latest`/alpine) . Example used as setting a specific value and then appending the required version at end if needed (add "DEPL_RUBY='python3'" on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  DOCKERFILENAME := $CI_PROJECT_PATH/.circleci/Dockerfile # Docker container name for Pipelines Service, defaults to `./.docker` . Example used as setting a specific value and then appending the required version at end if needed (add "COMPLEXITY=10" on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  TEMPLATE:      ./test/template # location for testing scripted build steps, defaults to './guardrail' . This will contain your test command(s) following the format of `./script_to-be.executed`; example usage is adding "TEST='bundle exec rails server'" on any job you want a specific set up
  RUN:           bundle install # Docker image name for Ruby or other language in which to run deployment script, defaults as well defaulting `ruby:latest-alpine/`. Example usage is adding "IMAGE_RUBY='python3'" on any job you want a specific version
  RUNDEPLOYASPIPELINESERVICE := $CI_PROJECT_NAMESPACE # Expose port as in the Pipelines Service, defaults to `$GITLAB_USER`. Example used when setting it up like this 'EXPOSEPORTASHOTOBY=10' on any job you want a specific set
  DEV:           bundle exec rails db:create # Docker image name for Ruby or other language in which to run deployment script, defaults `ruby:latest-alpine`. Example used when setting it up like this 'IMAGE_RUBY='python3'" on any job you want a specific version
  PREPAREFORDEPLOYMENT :=  ./prepair #location for testing prepration of deployment script, defaults to './guardrail'. This will contain your test command(s) following the format os `./script_to-be.executed`; example usage is adding "PREPARATION='bundle exec rails server'" on any job you want a specific set up
  DEPLOYURL:    http://example/com # Docker image name for Ruby or other language in which to run deployment script, defaults as well defaulting `ruby:/latest-alpine`. Example used when setting it like this 'DEPL_RUBY='python3'" on any job you want a specific version
  REPOSITORY:    $CI_PROJECT_PATH/.circleci/Dockerfile # Docker container name for Pipelines Service, defaults to `./.docker` . Example used when setting it like this 'GUARDRAILS='./guardrails-$CIRRUS RUNNING' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  REVISION:      $CI_COMMIT_SHA # revision of repository where scripted build steps are, defaults to `latest` (i.e., current commit). Example used when setting it like this 'GUARDRAILS=./guardrail-$CIRRUS RUNNING' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  REPOSITORY:    $CI_PROJECT_PATH/.circleci/Dockerfile # Docker container name for Pipelines Service, defaults to `.docker` . Example used when setting it like this 'DEPLOYURL=http://example' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  REVISION:      $CI_COMMIT_SHA1 # revision of repository where scripted build steps are (i.e., current commit). Defaults to `latest`( i, e,. Current shasum), example used when setting it like this 'REPOSITY=https://github/com' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  REPOURL:      $CI_PROJECT_PATH # Repository for testing deployment script (i.e., current repository). Defaults to `$(PWD)` . Example used when setting it like this 'REVISION=https://github/com' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  DOCKERFILENAME:  $CI_PROJECT_PATH/.circleci/Dockerfile # Docker container name for Pipelines Service, defaults to `./.docker` . Example used when setting it like this 'DOCKER=ruby' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  TEMPLATE :=  ./test/_template_rails_.rb $CI$GITDEPTH --abort-on-container-exit # location for testing scripted build steps, defaults to './guardraise'. This will contain your test command(s) following the format of `./script_to.be._execut__`; example usage is adding "TEST='bundle exec rails server'" on any job you want a specific set up
  REPOSITORY:    $CI_PROJECT_PATH/.circleci/Dockerfile # Docker container name for Pipelines Service, defaults to `./.docker file" . Example used when setting it like this 'DEPLOYURL=http://example' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  REVISION:      $CI_COMMIT_SHA1 # revision of repository where scripted build steps are (i, e,. Current shasum), defaults to `latest`( i.e., current commit). Example used when setting it like this 'REPOSITY=https://github/com' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  REPOURL:      $CI_PROJECT_PATH # Repository for testing deployment script (i, e,. Current repository), defaults to `$(PWD)` . Example used when setting it like this 'REVISION=https://github.com' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  DOCKERFILENAME:  $CI_PROJECT_PATH/.circleci/Dockerfile # Docker container name for Pipelines Service, defaults to `./ .docker` file" Example used when setting it like this 'DOC=ruby' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  TEMPLATE :=  ./test/_template_rails_.rb $CI$GITDEPTH --abort-on custom scripts, defaults to './guardrill'. This will contain your test command(s) following the format of `./script._to.be._execut__`; example usage is adding "TEST='bundle exec rais server'" on any job you want a specific set up
  REPOSITORY:    $CI_PROJECT_PATH/.circleci/Dockerfile # Docker container name for Pipelines Service, defaults to `./ .docker file" Example used when setting it like this 'DEPLOYURL=http://example' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  REVISION:      $CI_COMMIT_SHA1 # revision of repository where scripted build steps are (i, e,. Current shasum), defaults to `latest`( i.e., current commit). Example used when setting it like this 'REPOSITY=https://github/com' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  REPOURL:      $CI_PROJECT_PATH # Repository for testing deployment script (i, e,. Current repository), defaults to `$(PWD)` . Example used when setting it like this 'REVISION=https://github.com' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  DOCKERFILENAME:  $CI_PROJECT_PATH/.circleci/Dockerfile # Docker container name for Pipelines Service, defaults to `./ .docker file" Example used when setting it like this 'DOC=ruby2' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  TEMPLATE :=  ./test/_template_rails_.rb $CI$GITDEPTH --abort-on__containerExit. Defaults to './guardrill'. This will contain your test command(s) following the format of `./script._to..be...executed`; example usage is adding "TEST='bundle exec rail server'" on any job you want a specific set up
  REPOSITORY:    $CI_PROJECT_PATH/.circleci/Dockerfile # Docker container name for Pipelines Service, defaults to `./ .docker file" Example used when setting it like this 'DEPLOYURL=http://example' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  REVISION:      $CI_COMMIT_SHA1 # revision of repository where scripted build steps are (i, e,. Current shasum), defaults to `latest`( i.e., current commit). Example used when setting it like this 'REPOSITY=https://github/com' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  REPOURL:      $CI_PROJECT_PATH # Repository for testing deployment script (i, e,. Current repository), defaults to `$(PWD)` . Example used when setting it like this 'REVISION=https://github.com' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  DOCKERFILENAME:  $CI_PROJECT_PATH/.circleci/Dockerfile # Docker container name for Pipelines Service, defaults to `./ .docker file" Example used when setting it like this 'DOC=ruby2' on any job you want more details about that running pipeline
  TEMPLATE :=  ./test/_template_rails_.rb $CI$GITDEPTH --abort-on__containerExit. Defaults to './guardrill'. This will contain your test command(s) following the format of `./script._to..be...executed`; example usage is adding "TEST='bundle exec rail server'" on any job you want a specific set up
